_meta.description: Proxy rules for asset classes that did not exist (or had de minimis market cap) during a historical scenario. Maps the missing class to a similar-behavior class with an optional leverage factor.

crypto_major::gfc_2008.rationale: Bitcoin was launched January 2009; no market existed during GFC. Proxied by US tech equity scaled 1.5x to reflect higher historical volatility profile.

crypto_alt.rationale: Most altcoin markets emerged 2014–2017. Proxied by tech equity scaled 2.0x for higher beta and lower liquidity.

crypto_major::flash_crash_2010.rationale: Bitcoin existed in May 2010 but had de minimis volume; 2010 prices reflect cottage-industry conditions, not a meaningful market reaction. Proxied by tech equity scaled 1.2x.

crypto_alt::flash_crash_2010.rationale: Altcoins did not exist in 2010. Proxied by tech equity scaled 1.5x.

fallback._doc: Used when an asset_class has no shock entry AND no proxy rule for the scenario. Conservative broad-market downside assumption.
fallback.label: Broad market stress assumption (data unavailable)
